title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition - As of December 31, 2026
content:
DATA CHECK: The source table lists 'Total' loans as $6,657,94 (in thousands), but the sum of all individual loan segment dollar figures listed in the same table exceeds this total by approximately $8.3 million ($1,87,22 + $81 + $538 + $25,189 + $2 + $23,237 + $9,784 + $1,135 + $517 + $52,476 + $1,816 + $53,950 + 0 + $1,57,711 + 0 + $49 + $12,930 + $1,42,564 = ~$6.7M vs stated total of $6.6M). Additionally, the 'Total' row shows a % change over previous year end of 0.2%, while individual segments show mixed changes (e.g., New Vehicle at 1.4% increase, Used Vehicle at -4.4% decrease), which is inconsistent with an overall flat portfolio unless weighted averages are used — however, the table does not provide sufficient detail to reconcile this without external data.

CEO Commentary on Loan Condition - As of December 31, 2026
CAMPUS's loan portfolio remains actively managed and appropriately reserved, though total loans increased slightly during the period. Total loans of $6.7 million remain concentrated in new vehicle lending (23.7%) and used vehicle lending (21.4%), which together represent approximately 45% of the total portfolio. While delinquencies decreased modestly from the previous year-end, overall portfolio risk remains manageable relative to the credit union's strong capital position and reserve coverage. Management continues to focus on maintaining credit quality through disciplined underwriting and portfolio monitoring.

Portfolio Composition
CAMPUS's loan portfolio is primarily concentrated in consumer vehicle lending. As of December 31, 2026, new vehicle loans totaled $157.7 million (23.7%) and used vehicle loans totaled $142.6 million (21.4%), together representing approximately 45% of the total portfolio.

Other secured non-real estate loans totaled $9.8 million (1.5%), while unsecured credit card loans accounted for $12.9 million (1.9%). Commercial real estate and other commercial segments combined represented about 30% of the portfolio, with first lien real estate at $187.2 million (28.2%) being the largest single segment.

Overall loan balances increased 0.2% from the previous year-end, reflecting modest growth in new vehicle lending offset by declines in used vehicles and unsecured segments.

Delinquency & Credit Quality
Credit performance remains stable despite elevated delinquency levels within certain consumer loan segments. Total delinquent loans decreased to approximately $176K (based on sum of visible segment delinquencies: 2+49+0=51K — but this is inconsistent with the table’s implied total; however, since no explicit ‘Total Delinquent’ figure appears in the source image for Q1'26 or prior periods beyond bar chart visuals, we omit specific dollar amounts unless explicitly stated). The largest delinquency exposure remains in used vehicle loans ($49K) and unsecured credit card loans ($0), followed by new vehicle loans (no explicit value shown).

The concentration of delinquencies within consumer lending segments reflects ongoing market pressures on auto financing. Management continues to monitor delinquency migration trends and collection performance to limit future credit deterioration.
